qas.q: Who needs BBP training according to OSHA?
qas.a: Healthcare workers, daycare workers, and tattoo artist

qas.a: Sexual contact, illegal drug use, needle sticks

qas.a: Keep it covered with a bandage

qas.a: Attacks the body ability to protect itself against disease and it causes AIDS

What are some signs and symptoms of HBV
qas.a: Jaundice, tiredness, loss of appetite, dark urine

qas.a: Controls that reduce the likelihood of exposure by altering the manner in which you do a task

Eliminate- eliminate the hazardous task if possible
Substitute- substitute the task with a safer method if possible

qas.q: One way to protect against BBP's is control hazards. List the different aspects of this practice.
qas.a: A solution of 1 part household bleach to 9 parts water (10:1 solution)

qas.q: CDC recommends what to disinfect and clean body fluids?
qas.a: Gloves, removal visible contaminate, spray disinfect and let stand for several minutes then wipe, dry area

qas.q: What is the procedure for disinfecting?
qas.a: Controls that isolate or remove bloodborne pathogen hazard from workplace
Wear gloves and personal protective equipment, follow all universal precautions and workplace regulations
